user_query:
I’m consolidating potential interview contacts for a study on interracial relationships. Please build and run a small command-line script that parses both a saved web sign-up page and a set of email messages, filters and de-duplicates candidates by consent and completeness, and produces a merged contact list plus follow-up drafts.

Inputs (all provided here under the workspace):
- input/signups.html (a saved web page with a table of sign-ups)
- input/inbox/*.eml (plain-text email messages from interested participants)

Requirements:
1) Write a command-line script (e.g., Python is fine) at scripts/merge_contacts.py that:
   - Reads and extracts structured data from input/signups.html by parsing the table with columns: Name, Email, Self-Identified Race(s), Partner Race(s), Availability, City, Consent, Message.
   - Reads all .eml files in input/inbox/ and extracts structured fields from the message body if present (Name, Email, Self-Identified Race(s), Partner Race(s), Availability, Consent, Message), along with the email Subject header as thread_subject.
   - Normalizes and merges records by email (case-insensitive):
     • Keep only records with Consent == "Yes" (case-insensitive) and where both self-identified race(s) and partner race(s) are not empty.
     • If the same email appears in both sources, treat it as one record with source = "both". Prefer the form’s values for demographics (self-identified race(s), partner race(s)) and availability if present; keep the email’s subject as thread_subject; city comes only from the form.
     • If a record appears in only one source, set source to "form" or "email" accordingly.
     • For race fields, split on commas, semicolons, or slashes, trim whitespace, and re-join as a single string using "; " between items.
     • message_preview: use the participant’s message text truncated to the first 60 characters (for form records, use the Message column; for email records, use the value from a "Message:" line if present, otherwise the first 60 non-whitespace characters of the email body). Do not include the field labels in the preview.
2) After implementing the script, run it locally to produce these outputs:
   - output/merged_contacts.csv with a header row and the following columns in this order:
     full_name, email, self_identified_races, partner_races, availability, city, consent, source, thread_subject, message_preview
   - One follow-up draft per included contact in output/followups/, named using the email local-part (before the "@") plus ".txt" (e.g., alex.johnson.txt). Each file must start with:
     • A line: "To: <email>"
     • A line: "Subject: Interview Scheduling – <full_name>"
     and include a brief body referencing their self-identified race(s), their partner’s race(s), and their availability. Keep it 2–4 short sentences.
   - output/summary.txt summarizing processing counts on separate lines: (a) total records parsed from the form, (b) total emails parsed, (c) number consenting, (d) number excluded for missing race fields, (e) number after de-duplication, and (f) the list of included emails (one per line, sorted alphabetically).

Notes:
- Deduplicate strictly by lowercase email address.
- Treat empty cells as missing; trim whitespace.
- The script should be idempotent and overwrite outputs if re-run.

Please commit the script at scripts/merge_contacts.py, run it on the provided inputs, and ensure the three outputs are created exactly at: output/merged_contacts.csv, output/followups/, and output/summary.txt. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
